⭐ Health and Physical Education

Food and Nutrition

Discover what to eat, why to eat it, and how food affects your body!

📚 Unit 3 ⏱️ 9 min read

Unit 3: Food and Nutrition

Food gives us energy to work, play, and grow. Nutrition is how our body uses food to stay healthy.

Lesson 1: Nutrition and Nutrients

What is Nutrition?

Nutrition is the process of taking in food and using it for growth, energy, and health.

Why Do We Need Food?

ReasonExplanation
EnergyTo walk, run, play, and study
GrowthTo grow taller and stronger
RepairTo heal cuts and injuries
ProtectionTo fight diseases
WarmthTo keep body temperature normal

What are Nutrients?

Nutrients are the substances in food that our body needs to function properly.

The Six Main Nutrients

NutrientFunctionSourcesDeficiency effect
CarbohydratesGive energyRice, bread, potato, cornWeakness, tiredness
ProteinsBuild and repair bodyEggs, meat, fish, beans, milkSlow growth, weak muscles
FatsStore energy, keep warmOil, butter, nuts, gheeDry skin, lack of energy
VitaminsProtect from diseasesFruits, vegetablesVarious deficiency diseases
MineralsStrong bones and teethMilk, salt, greens, nutsWeak bones, anemia
WaterCarry nutrients, remove wasteWater, juices, soupsDehydration

Important Vitamins and Minerals

Vitamin/MineralFunctionSourcesDeficiency disease
Vitamin AGood eyesightCarrots, papaya, mangoNight blindness
Vitamin BEnergy productionRice, beans, meatBeriberi
Vitamin CImmunity, healingLemon, orange, amlaScurvy (bleeding gums)
Vitamin DStrong bonesSunlight, milk, eggsRickets (weak bones)
CalciumStrong bones and teethMilk, cheese, greensWeak bones
IronMakes blood redSpinach, dates, meatAnemia (weakness)
IodineThyroid functionIodized saltGoiter (swollen neck)

Balanced Diet

A balanced diet contains all nutrients in the right amounts.

The Food Plate (Healthy Eating Guide)

┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │ ┌──────────┐ │ │ │ Fruits │ │ │ ┌───────┤ & Veg ├───────┐ │ │ │ └──────────┘ │ │ │ │ Grains │ │ │ │ (Rice, │ │ │ │ Bread, │ │ │ │ Cereals) │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ ┌──────────────┐ │ │ │ │ │ Proteins │ │ │ │ └────┤ (Meat, ├──────┘ │ │ │ Beans, │ │ │ │ Eggs, │ │ │ │ Milk) │ │ │ └──────────────┘ │ │ │ │ Water (6-8 glasses) │ └─────────────────────────────────────┘

Malnutrition

Malnutrition means not getting the right amount of nutrients.

TypeMeaningExample
UndernutritionNot enough foodEating very little
OvernutritionToo much foodEating too much junk food
Micronutrient deficiencyMissing vitamins/mineralsNo fruits or vegetables

Effects of Malnutrition:

  • Weakness and tiredness
  • Slow growth
  • Frequent sickness
  • Poor concentration in school
  • Weight loss or obesity

Lesson 2: Locally Available Food Items

Locally available food means food grown or produced near your home. It is often fresh, cheap, and healthy.

Types of Locally Available Food in Nepal (and similar regions)

TypeExamplesNutrients
GrainsRice, maize, millet, wheat, barleyCarbohydrates
LegumesLentils (dal), beans, chickpeas, soybeansProteins
VegetablesSpinach, cauliflower, cabbage, pumpkin, radish, potatoVitamins, minerals, fiber
FruitsMango, orange, banana, apple, papaya, guavaVitamins, minerals
Milk productsMilk, curd, ghee, paneerProteins, calcium, fats
Meat and eggsChicken, fish, goat meat, eggsProteins
Oils and fatsMustard oil, ghee, butterFats
SpicesTurmeric, cumin, coriander, ginger, garlicMedicinal properties

Seasonal Fruits and Vegetables

SeasonFruitsVegetables
SummerMango, watermelon, lychee, jackfruitCucumber, pumpkin, bitter gourd
RainyPears, plumCauliflower, cabbage
WinterOrange, apple, guava, grapesSpinach, peas, carrot, radish

💡 Why eat seasonal food? Seasonal food is fresher, tastier, cheaper, and has more nutrients.

Traditional vs. Modern Food

Traditional (Local) FoodModern (Processed) Food
Freshly cookedPackaged
No preservativesContains preservatives
Less oil and sugarOften high in oil, sugar, salt
CheaperExpensive
Examples: Dal-bhat, roti, vegetablesExamples: Noodles, chips, biscuits

Benefits of Eating Local Food

BenefitExplanation
FreshPicked or produced recently
NutritiousNutrients are still intact
CheapNo transport or packaging costs
Supports local farmersYour money helps local people
Environment-friendlyLess transport = less pollution

Lesson 3: Harmful Effects of Junk Food

Junk food is food that has little or no nutritional value. It is often high in sugar, salt, fat, and calories.

Common Junk Foods

TypeExamples
Fried foodsChips, french fries, samosa, pakora
Sugary drinksSoda, packaged juice, energy drinks
Fast foodBurger, pizza, noodles
SweetsCandy, chocolate, cake, ice cream
Processed snacksBiscuits, packaged cakes, instant noodles

Why is Junk Food Harmful?

ProblemExplanation
ObesityHigh calories with no nutrition lead to weight gain
Tooth decaySugar feeds bacteria that damage teeth
Weak bonesJunk food has no calcium
Digestion problemsLow fiber causes constipation
Low energyYou feel tired and lazy after eating junk food
Poor concentrationCannot focus in class
Skin problemsPimples and acne
DiseasesRisk of diabetes, heart disease later in life

Junk Food vs. Healthy Food

Junk Food ❌Healthy Food ✅
Potato chipsRoasted chickpeas or nuts
SodaFresh lemon water or coconut water
BurgerWhole grain bread with vegetables
Ice creamFresh fruit or yogurt
Instant noodlesHome-cooked soup with vegetables

Tips to Avoid Junk Food

TipHow to do it
Pack your own snackBring fruits, nuts, or a sandwich to school
Drink waterInstead of soda, drink water when thirsty
Eat before going outDo not go to the market hungry
Ask for healthy optionsAt parties, choose fruits over cake
Read labelsAvoid foods with too much sugar or salt
Cook at homeHome food is healthier than outside food

Healthy Alternatives to Junk Food Cravings

If you want…Eat this instead…
Something sweetMango, banana, dates
Something saltyRoasted peanuts, salted popcorn
Something crunchyCarrot sticks, cucumber slices
Something coldFresh fruit juice, buttermilk

💡 Remember: Junk food is okay once in a while (on special occasions), but not every day!

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. The six main nutrients are carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, minerals, and ______.
  2. A ______ contains all nutrients in the right amounts.
  3. Vitamin A deficiency causes ______ blindness.
  4. Junk food is high in ______, sugar, and oil.
  5. ______ deficiency causes anemia.

Exercise 2: Match the Nutrient to Its Function

NutrientFunction
Carbohydratesa) Builds and repairs body
Proteinsb) Gives energy
Vitamin Cc) Strong bones
Calciumd) Boosts immunity

Exercise 3: Healthy or Junk?

Write “Healthy” or “Junk”.

  1. Apple ______
  2. Chips ______
  3. Soda ______
  4. Milk ______
  5. Dal and rice ______

Exercise 4: Name the Deficiency Disease

  1. Lack of Vitamin A → ____________
  2. Lack of Iron → ____________
  3. Lack of Iodine → ____________
  4. Lack of Vitamin D → ____________

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. water, 2. balanced diet, 3. night, 4. salt/fat, 5. Iron

Exercise 2: Carbohydrates→b, Proteins→a, Vitamin C→d, Calcium→c

Exercise 3: 1. Healthy, 2. Junk, 3. Junk, 4. Healthy, 5. Healthy

Exercise 4: 1. Night blindness, 2. Anemia, 3. Goiter, 4. Rickets

Great work! Eat healthy, stay healthy. Your body is your most important possession – feed it well!
